title: gardenia resiniflua subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Gardenia resiniflua subsp. septentrionalis, also known as the northern gardenia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gardenia family, Rutaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal and aromatic properties. The leaves and flowers of the northern gardenia have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Northern gardenia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation, improve immune function, and provide antioxidant benefits.
